mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for developing agentic workflows, ideal for teams looking to streamline their AI application development. It provides a robust set of features for self-hosting and can be integrated into existing Kubernetes environments. Key trade-offs include the need for infrastructure management and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dify Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.langgenius.com

helm repo update

```

> This adds the Dify Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dify to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This applies the CRDs necessary for Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Dify into your Kubernetes cluster, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> Replace with the desired version if necessary.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Dify, which is necessary for it to operate.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

```

> This creates a sample Dify resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: Dify
metadata:
  name: example-dify
spec:
  workflow: example-workflow
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Dify pods to ensure that everything is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> You should see the Dify pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This command removes Dify from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This removes the Dify release.
> This deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process will upgrade Dify to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> Drains the nodes running Dify pods.
> Replace with the desired version.
> Verify that the Dify pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n dify -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dify CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the Dify configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dify' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The database service may not be running or accessible.

**Fix:**
Ensure the database service is running and check the connection settings in the Dify configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Resource not found: 'example-dify'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Custom Resource may not exist.

**Fix:**
Create the Custom Resource again using the provided YAML configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new workflow management features and improved API performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update existing workflows to align with the new configuration schema.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify pods running with the status 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n dify'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
